title: Replace flux install with FluxInstance in kind Quick Start
description:
# GitHub Issue #251: Replace imperative `flux install` bootstrap with flux-operator FluxInstance in kind Quick Start

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Today the kind bootstrap (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:597-600`) installs FluxCD imperatively with `flux install`, which pulls the `flux-system` manifest bundle and applies it directly to the API server. The Quick Start prerequisites table (`docs/quick-start.md:38`) therefore lists the Flux CLI as a hard requirement, and `hack/install-test-deps.sh:17,35-41` pins `FLUX_VERSION="2.5.1"` with four per-platform SHA-256 hashes that have to be rotated on every Flux release. This contradicts the architecture target already documented in `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/index.md:6,11-23` and `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md:18-33`, which both declare the [ControlPlane flux-operator](https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator) (`fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1`) as the production deployment model for Flux itself and enumerate its advantages over `flux bootstrap`/`flux install` (declarative lifecycle, automatic SemVer upgrades via `distribution.version: \"2.x\"`, `FluxReport` status surface, no circular \"Flux deploys itself\" dependency).

Switch the kind bootstrap to the same mechanism: (1) replace Step 2 of `hack/deploy-infra.sh` with `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator/releases/download/v<pin>/install.yaml` followed by `kubectl apply -f deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` and a wait on `FluxInstance/flux` `Ready`; (2) add `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` with a minimal `FluxInstance` spec (`distribution.version: \"2.x\"`, `distribution.registry: ghcr.io/fluxcd`, `components: [source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller]`, `cluster.type: kubernetes`, `cluster.size: small`, `cluster.multitenant: false`, `cluster.networkPolicy: false`, no `sync` block — the Quick Start applies the kustomize overlay directly, matching today's behaviour); (3) replace the `flux reconcile source helm ...` loop (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:611-616`) with the operator-native annotation pattern `kubectl annotate helmrepository/<name> reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt=\"$(date +%s)\" --overwrite`, which drops the last hard dependency on the Flux CLI; (4) demote the Flux CLI in `hack/install-test-deps.sh` and the Quick Start prerequisites (`docs/quick-start.md:38`) to an optional debug tool (`flux get helmreleases`, `flux logs` are still convenient but no longer required for bootstrap). Pin flux-operator's own version via a release tag embedded in the `install.yaml` URL (latest as of 2026-04-21 is `v0.47.0`, released 2026-04-15) and manage future bumps through renovate the same way `deploy/flux-system/sources/*.yaml` chart versions are already managed (see `renovate.json`).

**Motivation**: The architecture docs have declared flux-operator the production-target pattern since the initial GitOps design (`architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/index.md`), but the kind Quick Start — which is the single on-ramp every new contributor touches first — still boots Flux the imperative way. Three concrete pain points fall out of this gap: (a) the pinned `FLUX_VERSION="2.5.1"` plus four SHA-256 hashes in `hack/install-test-deps.sh` have to be rotated by hand on every Flux minor/patch release because the CLI is not in scope for any automation; with the operator, Flux itself is managed by a SemVer range (`2.x`) in the `FluxInstance` CRD and upgrades land without touching the repo; (b) contributors copy the Quick Start verbatim into their own laptops and then ask in review \"where do I see what Flux is doing?\" — the operator answers that with `kubectl get fluxreport -n flux-system flux -o yaml`, a first-class Kubernetes status surface, whereas `flux install` only exposes the standard Flux metrics; (c) the Quick Start claims the result is \"identical to what CI validates\" (`docs/quick-start.md:9-10`) and \"consistent with the production flux-system overlay\" (`docs/quick-start.md:141-142`), but that is only half true — the production overlay will use flux-operator while the kind path uses the native CLI, so the two diverge on exactly the layer the bootstrap teaches. Closing this gap also shrinks the toolchain surface (one less pinned binary to verify on every release), removes the circular \"flux installs itself\" story from the docs, and makes `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` materially shorter because the flux-system namespace is now managed by a CRD rather than a static manifest set.

**Affected Areas**:
- `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` (new — `FluxInstance` CR)
- `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` (add `fluxinstance.yaml` to `resources`)
- `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml` (ensure `flux-system` namespace is present for the operator install, which it already is implicitly via `flux install` today)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (replace `flux install` block at lines 597-600; replace `flux reconcile source helm` loop at lines 611-616; add a wait on `FluxInstance/flux` `Ready` before Step 3 applies the overlay)
- `hack/install-test-deps.sh` (drop `FLUX_VERSION` and `FLUX_SHA256_*` hard requirement; move Flux CLI to an optional install block with a clear \"used for debugging only\" note)
- `hack/teardown-infra.sh` (no change if it only runs `kind delete cluster`; verify)
- `docs/quick-start.md` (prerequisites table row for Flux CLI → optional; Step 3 internal-table row 2 rewrite; architecture-snapshot block adds `flux-system   flux-operator-* / source-controller-* / kustomize-controller-* / helm-controller-* / notification-controller-*`; add a tip pointing at `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system`)
- `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` (document `fluxinstance.yaml` alongside the existing `kustomization.yaml` / `namespaces.yaml` entries; explain that the `flux-system` namespace contents are now operator-managed)
- `docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md` (Step 2 rename: \"Install flux-operator + apply FluxInstance\" instead of \"Install FluxCD (flux install)\"; line 99 and surrounding references)
- `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` (cross-link the kind overlay now matches the described bootstrap flow, remove the hedging wording)
- `.github/workflows/*.yml` (audit every CI step that runs `flux ...` — only `flux reconcile` usage in `deploy-infra.sh` is in scope; adjust if any workflow calls `flux` directly)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` exists with `apiVersion: fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1`, `kind: FluxInstance`, `metadata.name: flux`, `metadata.namespace: flux-system`, and the spec outlined in the description (no `sync` block — the kind overlay applies Helm sources/releases directly via kustomize)
- [ ] `hack/deploy-infra.sh` Step 2 installs flux-operator via `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator/releases/download/<pinned-tag>/install.yaml`, applies `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml`, and waits for `FluxInstance/flux` `condition=Ready` with the existing `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` envelope
- [ ] The `<pinned-tag>` is configurable via a script-level variable (e.g. `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION=\"v0.47.0\"`) so renovate or a manual bump can update it in one place
- [ ] The `flux reconcile source helm` loop in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is replaced by `kubectl annotate helmrepository/<name> reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt=\"$(date +%s%N)\" --overwrite` over the same list of sources
- [ ] `hack/install-test-deps.sh` no longer fails when the Flux CLI is absent; if the user opts into installing it (e.g. `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true`), the pinned SHA-256 verification still applies
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster ends with `FluxInstance/flux` `Ready=True`, a `FluxReport/flux` populated (`.status.distribution.entitlement` present), and the same set of HelmReleases Ready as today (cert-manager, openbao, mariadb-operator-crds, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator, chaos-mesh, keystone-operator)
- [ ] `make teardown-infra` works unchanged
- [ ] `docs/quick-start.md` prerequisites table marks Flux CLI as **Optional** (debugging only); Step 3 internal table row 2 reads \"**Install flux-operator** + apply `FluxInstance/flux`\"; the \"What gets deployed\" details block lists the flux-system pods
- [ ] `docs/quick-start.md` gains a short tip near Step 3 showing `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system -o yaml` as the first-stop diagnostic for Flux itself
- [ ] `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` documents `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` in the same shape as the other manifest entries
- [ ] E2E in CI still passes: the kind-based `make test-e2e` and the chaos job (which depends on `tests/e2e-chaos/unseal-openbao.sh`) complete without regression; the Tempest job (`hack/run-tempest.sh`) is unaffected because it only touches `keystone-api.openstack.svc`
- [ ] The renovate config keeps working — the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` pin (either in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` or a dedicated `.version` file) is picked up by a renovate `customManagers` entry analogous to the existing `releases/*/source-refs.yaml` manager in `renovate.json`, so the tag bump happens automatically
- [ ] `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` no longer describes the kind path as a deviation — the \"Advantages over `flux bootstrap`\" table at `01-gitops-fluxcd/index.md:15-23` now applies to both production and kind

**Non-Goals**:
- Enabling a git `sync` source on the `FluxInstance`. The kind overlay is self-contained and the Quick Start should stay a zero-token, zero-SSH-key experience. The git-sync flow is described for the production Management Cluster in `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` and will land in a separate change once the `c5c3-gitops` repository is in scope.
- Migrating the image-automation / image-reflector controllers on. Today's `flux install` default does not include them either, so this is a strictly no-op alignment on components.
- Enabling `cluster.multitenant: true` / `cluster.networkPolicy: true` on the kind overlay. The kind cluster is single-tenant demo scope and network policies would require additional plumbing around the existing Keystone `NetworkPolicy` sub-reconciler (issue #127); out of scope here.
- Switching the production flux-system overlay to flux-operator. That is a separate, coordinated change on the actual Management Cluster with its own GitOps repo; this issue is scoped to the kind Quick Start and the manifests under `deploy/flux-system/` that kind shares with it.
- Removing the Flux CLI from `hack/install-test-deps.sh` entirely. The CLI is still useful for contributors debugging `HelmRelease` reconcile loops (`flux logs`, `flux trace`), so it stays installable on request — it just stops being a hard prerequisite of `make deploy-infra`.

**References**:
- flux-operator repository: https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator
- Install guide: https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/guides/install/
- FluxInstance CRD: https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/crd/fluxinstance.md
- FluxReport CRD: https://fluxoperator.dev/docs/crd/fluxreport.md
- Latest release at authoring time: `v0.47.0` (2026-04-15)

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/251

stories.title: Quick Start contributor no longer needs the Flux CLI to bootstrap kind
stories.want: to run `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster without first installing the Flux CLI
stories.so_that: the on-ramp matches the architecture-target operator pattern and one fewer pinned binary is required
criteria: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` preflight does NOT abort when the `flux` binary is missing from PATH (CC-0085)
Running `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster with no `flux` binary on PATH ends with `FluxInstance/flux` showing `condition=Ready=True`
All HelmReleases (cert-manager, openbao, mariadb-operator-crds, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator, prometheus-operator-crds, chaos-mesh) become Ready exactly as today
`docs/quick-start.md` prerequisites table marks the Flux CLI row as **Optional** with a 'debugging only' annotation

stories.title: Operator inspects Flux installation health via FluxReport
stories.want: a single first-stop `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system -o yaml` command that summarises Flux controller health, distribution, and reconciler stats
stories.so_that: I can answer 'is Flux itself broken or is it just my HelmRelease?' without learning Flux CLI commands
criteria: After `make deploy-infra`, `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system` returns a populated resource with `.status.distribution.entitlement` set
`docs/quick-start.md` Step 3 contains a tip block showing the `kubectl get fluxreport/flux` command and a one-line description of what it reports
`docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` cross-links `FluxReport` from the new `fluxinstance.yaml` documentation entry
No reliance on the Flux CLI to inspect status (uses kubectl only)

stories.title: Maintainer rotates Flux versions through SemVer + renovate, not by hand
stories.want: Flux distribution upgrades to land via the `distribution.version: "2.x"` SemVer range and the flux-operator pin to land via renovate
stories.so_that: Flux version bumps no longer require editing four SHA-256 hashes in `hack/install-test-deps.sh` per release
criteria: `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` declares `spec.distribution.version: "2.x"` and `spec.distribution.registry: ghcr.io/fluxcd`
`hack/deploy-infra.sh` defines a single `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` variable (default pinned to a specific semver tag, e.g. `v0.47.0`) used to construct the `install.yaml` URL

`renovate.json` has a `customManagers` entry that recognises the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION="v<x.y.z>"` literal in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` and tracks the GitHub release tags of `controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator`
`hack/install-test-deps.sh` no longer requires `FLUX_VERSION` / `FLUX_SHA256_*` for the deploy path (they only matter when `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true`)

stories.title: CI runs the same bootstrap as the laptop path
stories.want: the e2e-infra, e2e-operator, e2e-chaos, and tempest jobs to keep working with the new bootstrap without any workflow surgery beyond what the composite action already does
stories.so_that: the existing `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra` composite action stays the single shared entrypoint
criteria: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health` Chainsaw test asserts `FluxInstance/flux` Ready=True and `FluxReport/flux` exists with `.status.distribution.entitlement` populated
All existing infra-stack-health assertions (cert-manager, ESO, MariaDB, Memcached, OpenBao, Chaos Mesh, ClusterIssuer) continue to pass unchanged
`hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` includes `kubectl get fluxinstance,fluxreport -A -o yaml` so failures show flux-operator status

The CI Flux-CLI install step (`fluxcd/flux2/action`) inside the composite action is removed OR explicitly retained for the diagnostics-only `flux logs` call (decision is captured in code with a one-line comment)

stories.title: Reader of the architecture/reference docs sees a coherent flux-operator story
stories.role: developer or reviewer reading the docs
stories.want: the kind Quick Start, the infrastructure manifests reference, and the bootstrap architecture doc to all describe the same flux-operator-based flow
stories.so_that: the production target and the kind on-ramp no longer diverge on the very layer that bootstraps everything else
criteria: `docs/quick-start.md` Step 3 internal table row 2 reads `**Install flux-operator** + apply `FluxInstance/flux`` and the 'What gets deployed' block lists the five flux-system pods (flux-operator, source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller)
`docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` has a new section documenting `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` (apiVersion, fields, defaults) in the same shape as the existing manifest entries
`docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md` Step 2 of the deployment sequence reads 'Install flux-operator + apply FluxInstance' and the prerequisites table marks `flux` CLI as Optional
`architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` no longer hedges that kind diverges — the bootstrap flow described there now matches the kind overlay

requirements.description: The system SHALL deploy flux-operator declaratively in Step 2 of `hack/deploy-infra.sh` instead of running `flux install`.
requirements.rationale: Aligns the kind Quick Start with the architecture-target operator pattern (CC-0085) and removes the hard dependency on the imperative Flux CLI for bootstrap.

scenarios.name: Fresh cluster bootstrap
scenarios.when: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` reaches Step 2 on a kind cluster with no Flux components installed
scenarios.then: the script runs `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator/releases/download/${FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION}/install.yaml` and the flux-operator Deployment becomes available
and_then: no invocation of the `flux` CLI happens during Step 2
the `flux-system` namespace contains the `flux-operator` Deployment within `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` seconds

scenarios.name: Re-run on an already-bootstrapped cluster
scenarios.when: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is re-run against the same kind cluster
scenarios.then: `kubectl apply -f .../install.yaml` is idempotent (server-side apply or Replace semantics) and the script proceeds without error
and_then: no duplicate flux-operator Deployment is created

scenarios.name: FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION pin is configurable
scenarios.when: the user exports `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION=v0.46.0` before running `make deploy-infra`
scenarios.then: the URL contains `v0.46.0` and that release of flux-operator is installed
and_then: the script does not hardcode the version anywhere except the default value of the variable

requirements.description: The system SHALL apply `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` and wait for `FluxInstance/flux` `condition=Ready=True` before proceeding to Step 3.
requirements.rationale: Without a readiness gate, subsequent kubectl apply of the kustomize overlay can race the flux controllers' CRD installation, causing intermittent HelmRelease apply failures.

scenarios.name: Happy path readiness
scenarios.when: the FluxInstance is applied and the flux-operator reconciles it
scenarios.then: the script polls `kubectl get fluxinstance/flux -n flux-system` until `condition[type=Ready].status=True`, with the same `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` envelope used for HelmReleases
and_then: the script logs progress every 10 seconds (matching `wait_for_helmreleases` cadence)

scenarios.name: Timeout produces actionable diagnostics
scenarios.when: FluxInstance does not reach Ready within `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT`
scenarios.then: the script logs `kubectl describe fluxinstance/flux` and `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -o yaml` then exits non-zero
and_then: the failure is distinguishable from HelmRelease timeouts in the log stream

scenarios.name: FluxInstance applied through kustomize
scenarios.when: `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` is included in `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` resources list
scenarios.then: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` renders the FluxInstance resource exactly once
and_then: kind overlay does not need its own patch for FluxInstance

requirements.description: The system SHALL replace the `flux reconcile source helm` loop with `kubectl annotate helmrepository/<name> reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt="$(date +%s%N)" --overwrite` over every HelmRepository in `flux-system`.
requirements.rationale: Removes the last hard dependency on the Flux CLI from `hack/deploy-infra.sh`; the operator-native annotation pattern is documented as the reconciliation trigger by the FluxCD project.

scenarios.name: All HelmRepositories get reconcile-trigger annotation
scenarios.when: the script reaches the source-reconcile block after Step 3
scenarios.then: every HelmRepository in `flux-system` is annotated with `reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt` carrying a fresh nanosecond-precision timestamp
and_then: the annotation overwrite is non-fatal if a repository disappears between list and annotate (mirrors the `|| true` semantics today)

scenarios.name: Empty repository list is a no-op
scenarios.when: no HelmRepositories exist in `flux-system` (e.g. base overlay has not been applied yet)
scenarios.then: the loop completes without error and the script proceeds

scenarios.name: Annotation triggers a fresh chart fetch
scenarios.when: a HelmRepository is annotated with a new `requestedAt` value
scenarios.then: the source-controller observes the annotation change and re-indexes the chart within one reconciliation interval
and_then: downstream HelmReleases proceed without waiting for the default 1h reconcile interval

requirements.description: The Flux CLI SHALL be optional in `hack/install-test-deps.sh`; the script MUST NOT fail when `WITH_FLUX_CLI` is unset or false.
requirements.rationale: Removes the last per-release hashing chore; preserves the CLI as an opt-in debugging aid (`flux logs`, `flux trace`) without making it a deploy prerequisite.

scenarios.name: Default install path skips Flux CLI
scenarios.when: a user runs `make install-test-deps` with no environment overrides
scenarios.then: chainsaw, kind, kubectl are installed and `install_flux` is NOT executed
and_then: the absence of `flux` in `${INSTALL_DIR}` is not an error

scenarios.name: Opt-in install of Flux CLI
scenarios.when: a user runs `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true make install-test-deps`
scenarios.then: the Flux CLI is downloaded, its pinned SHA256 hash is verified, and it is installed to `${INSTALL_DIR}/flux`
and_then: any FLUX_SHA256_* mismatch aborts the install (existing supply-chain hardening preserved)

scenarios.name: Skip-when-already-present semantics preserved
scenarios.when: `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true` is set and the correct Flux CLI version is already installed
scenarios.then: the install_flux function logs 'flux <version> already installed — skipping.' and returns without re-downloading

requirements.description: The `preflight_checks` function in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` SHALL NOT require the `flux` binary.
requirements.rationale: If `flux` is required by preflight, then making the install optional is meaningless — the deploy aborts before reaching Step 2.

scenarios.name: Preflight succeeds without flux on PATH
scenarios.when: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is invoked on a host where `command -v flux` returns non-zero
scenarios.then: the preflight loop iterates only over `docker kind kubectl jq` and prints 'Pre-flight checks passed.'

scenarios.name: Preflight still fails when kubectl is missing
scenarios.when: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is invoked on a host with no `kubectl` on PATH
scenarios.then: the script logs `ERROR: 'kubectl' is not installed or not in PATH.` and exits 1

scenarios.name: Diagnostics still use flux when present
scenarios.when: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` runs on failure and `flux` is on PATH
scenarios.then: the existing `flux logs --all-namespaces` block runs as today (best-effort)
and_then: no behaviour change when `flux` is absent — the existing `command -v` guard stays in place

requirements.description: `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` SHALL declare a minimal FluxInstance with no `sync` block.
requirements.rationale: The kind overlay applies the kustomize directly via `kubectl apply -k`, matching today's behaviour. A git sync source belongs to a separate change scoped to the production Management Cluster (per Non-Goals).

scenarios.name: Spec contents are exactly as scoped
scenarios.when: `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` is rendered through kustomize
scenarios.then: the resource has `apiVersion: fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1`, `kind: FluxInstance`, `metadata.name: flux`, `metadata.namespace: flux-system`, `spec.distribution.version: "2.x"`, `spec.distribution.registry: ghcr.io/fluxcd`, `spec.components: [source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller]`, `spec.cluster.type: kubernetes`, `spec.cluster.size: small`, `spec.cluster.multitenant: false`, `spec.cluster.networkPolicy: false`

scenarios.name: SPDX header is present
scenarios.when: the file is read
scenarios.then: the first three lines contain the SPDX-FileCopyrightText, blank comment, and SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 lines (matching project convention)

scenarios.name: FluxInstance referenced by name in flux-system namespace
scenarios.when: the kustomize overlay is applied
scenarios.then: exactly one FluxInstance named `flux` exists in the `flux-system` namespace
and_then: the existing namespace from `namespaces.yaml` is reused (no separate namespace creation needed for the operator install path because `kubectl apply -f .../install.yaml` creates it itself)

requirements.description: Renovate SHALL automatically detect updates to the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` pin in `hack/deploy-infra.sh`.
requirements.rationale: The whole point of moving to operator-managed Flux is that Flux distribution upgrades become automatic; the operator's own version still needs a managed bump path so that the chore does not silently move from `hack/install-test-deps.sh` (Flux CLI hashes) to `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION).

scenarios.name: Renovate identifies FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION as a versioned dependency
scenarios.when: renovate runs against the repository
scenarios.then: the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION="v<x.y.z>"` literal in `hack/deploy-infra.sh` is matched by a `customManagers` regex entry, and renovate reads the latest GitHub release tag of `controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator`
and_then: renovate opens a PR titled `chore(deps): update flux-operator to v<x.y.z>` when a newer tag is published

scenarios.name: Major bumps are gated like OpenStack tags
scenarios.when: renovate sees a major-version flux-operator release
scenarios.then: the package rule disables auto-update for major bumps (mirroring the `releases/**/source-refs.yaml` rule)

scenarios.name: Minor/patch bumps eligible for automerge after release age
scenarios.when: renovate sees a minor or patch flux-operator release
scenarios.then: the bump becomes eligible for automerge after a 3-day minimum release age (mirroring the OpenStack tags rule)

requirements.description: The Chainsaw E2E test suite `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health` SHALL assert FluxInstance and FluxReport health.
requirements.rationale: Without a Chainsaw assertion, a regression that broke the flux-operator install would only surface when a HelmRelease later failed — slowing diagnosis and weakening the e2e safety net.

scenarios.name: FluxInstance Ready assertion
scenarios.when: the infra-stack-health Chainsaw test runs after `make deploy-infra`
scenarios.then: the test asserts a `FluxInstance` named `flux` in `flux-system` with `condition[type=Ready].status=True`

scenarios.name: FluxReport populated assertion
scenarios.when: the infra-stack-health Chainsaw test runs
scenarios.then: the test asserts a `FluxReport` named `flux` exists in `flux-system` with `.status.distribution.entitlement` set to a non-empty string

scenarios.name: All five flux controllers running
scenarios.when: the infra-stack-health Chainsaw test runs
scenarios.then: the test asserts the Deployments `source-controller`, `kustomize-controller`, `helm-controller`, `notification-controller` AND the `flux-operator` Deployment all have `availableReplicas > 0` in `flux-system`

requirements.description: Documentation SHALL be updated to describe the new flux-operator bootstrap consistently.
requirements.rationale: Docs in three locations (Quick Start, infrastructure-manifests reference, e2e-deployment reference) describe the bootstrap; leaving any of them out of sync re-introduces the divergence the change is meant to close.

scenarios.name: Quick Start prerequisites mark Flux CLI optional
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/quick-start.md` Prerequisites section
scenarios.then: the Flux CLI row is annotated **Optional — debugging only** and the install snippet shows `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true make install-test-deps`

scenarios.name: Quick Start Step 3 internal table reflects new Step 2 wording
scenarios.when: a reader opens the internal step table in `docs/quick-start.md` Step 3
scenarios.then: row 2 reads `**Install flux-operator** + apply `FluxInstance/flux``
and_then: the 'What gets deployed' details block lists the `flux-system` pods (flux-operator, source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller)
a tip block shows `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system -o yaml` as the first-stop diagnostic

scenarios.name: Reference docs document fluxinstance.yaml
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md`
scenarios.then: a new section documents `deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml` (apiVersion, kind, fields, defaults), a row for `fluxinstance.yaml` is added to the directory layout block, and the resource counts table reflects the +1 resource
and_then: `docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md` Step 2 of the deployment sequence text reads 'Install flux-operator + apply FluxInstance', and the prerequisites table marks `flux` Optional
`architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` no longer hedges that kind diverges from the operator-based flow

requirements.description: The bootstrap SHOULD complete within the existing `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT` envelope on a fresh kind cluster.
requirements.rationale: Adding a new readiness gate (FluxInstance Ready) must not blow the e2e CI budget; current p95 is ~5–7 minutes for `make deploy-infra` on a 4-vCPU runner.

scenarios.name: End-to-end bootstrap stays within budget
scenarios.when: `make deploy-infra` runs on the `blacksmith-4vcpu-ubuntu-2404` runner used by e2e-infra
scenarios.then: the entire 8-step sequence completes within `HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT=600` seconds

scenarios.name: FluxInstance Ready reached well before timeout
scenarios.when: the FluxInstance readiness wait is observed standalone
scenarios.then: FluxInstance reaches Ready within 120 seconds of kubectl apply on a warm cache, 240 seconds on a cold image-pull

scenarios.name: Reconcile-trigger annotation is fast
scenarios.when: the new annotation loop replaces the `flux reconcile source helm` loop
scenarios.then: the per-repository annotate completes in <500ms (kubectl roundtrip), so the loop adds <5s for 7 repos

tasks.title: Create deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml with FluxInstance/flux spec (apiVersion fluxcd.controlplane.io/v1, distribution.version `2.x`, registry ghcr.io/fluxcd, four controllers, cluster.size=small, no sync block, SPDX header) (CC-0085, REQ-006)

Add `fluxinstance.yaml` to the resources list in deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml after `namespaces.yaml` and before sources/, and update the file header comment to mention the FluxInstance (CC-0085, REQ-006)

Add tests/unit/deploy/flux_system_fluxinstance_test.sh that runs `kustomize build deploy/flux-system/` and `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/`, asserting the FluxInstance shape and SPDX header (CC-0085, REQ-006)

In hack/deploy-infra.sh, add `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION="v0.47.0"` to the Configuration block; replace `flux install` (lines 597-600) with `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator/releases/download/${FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION}/install.yaml` followed by `kubectl apply -k deploy/flux-system/` (or apply the FluxInstance directly), and update the Step 2 banner text (CC-0085, REQ-001, REQ-002)

Add a new `wait_for_fluxinstance` helper in hack/deploy-infra.sh that polls `kubectl get fluxinstance/flux -n flux-system` for `condition[type=Ready].status=True` with the same 10s cadence and HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT envelope used by `wait_for_helmreleases`; on timeout, dump `kubectl describe fluxinstance/flux` and `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -o yaml` then exit 1 (CC-0085, REQ-002)

Replace the `flux reconcile source helm ... ` loop in hack/deploy-infra.sh (lines 611-616) with `kubectl annotate helmrepository/<name> reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt="$(date +%s%N)" --overwrite -n flux-system` over the same `kubectl get helmrepository -n flux-system` list (CC-0085, REQ-003)
Drop `flux` from the required-commands loop in `preflight_checks` in hack/deploy-infra.sh (line 314) so the script no longer aborts when the Flux CLI is absent (CC-0085, REQ-005)

Add bats-style unit tests under tests/unit/hack/ for the new shell behaviour: deploy_infra_preflight_test.bats (preflight passes without flux, fails without kubectl) and deploy_infra_reconcile_sources_test.bats (annotate-loop happy path + empty-list no-op, with kubectl stubbed via PATH) — wire into `make shellcheck`/`make test-shell` if a test runner exists, otherwise add a new make target `test-shell` (CC-0085, REQ-003, REQ-005)

Make install_flux opt-in in hack/install-test-deps.sh: gate the `install_flux` call in `main()` behind `[[ "${WITH_FLUX_CLI:-false}" == "true" ]]`; keep the FLUX_VERSION + FLUX_SHA256_* constants and the install_flux function intact, and update the script's leading comment to describe the new behaviour (CC-0085, REQ-004)

Add tests/unit/hack/install_test_deps_optional_flux_test.bats covering both gated paths (default skips install_flux; WITH_FLUX_CLI=true invokes install_flux and verify_sha256) using stubbed curl/install (CC-0085, REQ-004)

Add a `customManagers` entry to renovate.json that matches the literal `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION="v<x.y.z>"` in hack/deploy-infra.sh, with datasourceTemplate=github-releases, packageNameTemplate=controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator, and versioningTemplate=semver; add a packageRules entry that disables major bumps and automerges minor/patch after a 3-day minimumReleaseAge (mirroring the OpenStack tags rule) (CC-0085, REQ-007)

Add tests/unit/renovate/fluxoperator_custommanager_test.sh that runs `npx --yes --package renovate -- renovate-config-validator renovate.json` and a small grep-based smoke check that the regex captures the current FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION value (CC-0085, REQ-007)

Extend tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml with a new step that asserts FluxInstance/flux is Ready, FluxReport/flux exists with .status.distribution.entitlement set, and the five flux-system controller Deployments (flux-operator, source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller) have availableReplicas > 0 (CC-0085, REQ-008)

Add `kubectl get fluxinstance,fluxreport -A -o yaml` to hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh (after the existing `flux logs` block, gated only on the kubectl resource being registered) so failed CI runs surface flux-operator state (CC-0085, REQ-008)

Update docs/quick-start.md: prerequisites table marks Flux CLI Optional with `WITH_FLUX_CLI=true make install-test-deps` snippet; Step 3 internal table row 2 reads `**Install flux-operator** + apply FluxInstance/flux`; the `What gets deployed` details block lists the five flux-system pods; add a `::: tip` block near Step 3 with `kubectl get fluxreport/flux -n flux-system -o yaml` (CC-0085, REQ-009)

Update docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md: add `fluxinstance.yaml` to the Directory Layout block, add a new `## FluxInstance` section documenting apiVersion, fields, and defaults in the same shape as the other manifest entries, and increment the resource counts in the Base Kustomization table (CC-0085, REQ-009)

Update docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md: rename Step 2 of the deployment-sequence ASCII diagram to `Install flux-operator + apply FluxInstance`, mark the Flux CLI row in the Prerequisites table Optional, and add a one-line note explaining the `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` variable in the Environment Variables table (CC-0085, REQ-009)

Update architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md: remove the hedging that the kind path uses a different bootstrap, cross-link the kind overlay (deploy/flux-system/fluxinstance.yaml) and explain that kind uses no `spec.sync` block while production carries the GitRepository sync (CC-0085, REQ-009)

review_criteria: BLOCKER: `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster ends with FluxInstance/flux Ready=True and FluxReport/flux populated; all existing HelmReleases (cert-manager, openbao, mariadb-operator-crds, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, memcached-operator, prometheus-operator-crds, chaos-mesh) reach Ready
BLOCKER: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` no longer invokes the `flux` CLI in the deploy path (preflight, Step 2 install, source reconcile loop) — verified by grep
BLOCKER: `hack/install-test-deps.sh` succeeds with no `WITH_FLUX_CLI` set and never installs the Flux CLI in that mode
BLOCKER: `kustomize build deploy/flux-system/` and `kustomize build deploy/kind/base/` both produce exactly one FluxInstance/flux with the spec required by REQ-006
BLOCKER: All e2e test tiers pass: tests/unit/hack bats tests, tests/unit/deploy kustomize tests, tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health, tests/e2e-chaos suites, and tempest job
CRITICAL: `renovate.json` is accepted by `renovate-config-validator` and the new customManagers regex actually matches the current FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION literal in hack/deploy-infra.sh

CRITICAL: `docs/quick-start.md`, `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md`, `docs/reference/infrastructure/e2e-deployment.md` all reference the flux-operator path and mark the Flux CLI as Optional consistently
CRITICAL: `architecture/docs/05-deployment/01-gitops-fluxcd/04-bootstrap.md` no longer describes the kind path as a deviation from the operator-based bootstrap
CRITICAL: Bootstrap latency on the e2e-infra runner does not regress more than 30s vs. the `flux install` baseline (HELMRELEASE_TIMEOUT envelope unchanged at 600s)
MINOR: All new YAML files carry the SPDX-FileCopyrightText / SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 header (3 lines)
MINOR: Every new task, requirement, test, and doc reference includes the Feature ID `CC-0085`
MINOR: `make shellcheck` passes with no new warnings on hack/deploy-infra.sh and hack/install-test-deps.sh
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions:

1) flux-operator install URL form. The script applies the operator's published install.yaml directly via `kubectl apply -f https://github.com/controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator/releases/download/${FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION}/install.yaml` rather than via `helm install`. This avoids adding a Helm-CLI dependency to the deploy path and matches the operator project's quickstart documentation. The trade-off: the install.yaml is a static manifest, so the operator version is pinned by URL rather than by SemVer range. That is exactly what `FLUX_OPERATOR_VERSION` solves, and renovate is responsible for keeping it fresh.

2) FluxInstance lives in deploy/flux-system/, not in deploy/kind/. The same FluxInstance applies to kind today and to the future production overlay tomorrow — adding `spec.sync` later is a kind-vs-production overlay patch (Non-Goal here). Putting the file in the production root keeps it discoverable and aligns with the existing pattern (deploy/kind/base/ is purely a patch overlay).

3) Wait gate. A new `wait_for_fluxinstance` helper modelled on `wait_for_helmreleases` polls Ready=True. The helper is necessary because `kubectl wait` can race the CRD installation: if the FluxInstance CRD was installed by the same kubectl-apply that immediately preceded the wait, the API server may not yet have it registered when `kubectl wait` starts. Polling with `kubectl get` and absorbing transient `NotFound` errors mirrors the existing patterns in the file.

4) Reconcile-trigger annotation. The Flux source-controller documents `reconcile.fluxcd.io/requestedAt` as the operator-native trigger; updating the annotation forces a fresh reconcile within the next loop tick. Using `date +%s%N` (nanoseconds) instead of `date +%s` (seconds) avoids the case where the annotation value is unchanged across a sub-second loop.

5) Why no `cluster.networkPolicy: true`. Architecture has it set in the production design, but Non-Goals explicitly defer this for kind because Keystone's NetworkPolicy reconciler (issue #127) is out of scope here. Setting it false matches today's effective behaviour.

6) Composite action. `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` still installs the Flux CLI via `fluxcd/flux2/action`. Decision: keep the install step but add a one-line comment explaining it is for the diagnostics-only `flux logs` invocation in `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh`. Removing it would silently degrade the diagnostic output; tracking that as a follow-up keeps this change scoped.

7) Renovate customManagers shape. The existing entry uses `customType: regex`, `managerFilePatterns`, and a regex with named captures (depName, currentValue). The new entry follows the same shape, with `packageNameTemplate: "controlplaneio-fluxcd/flux-operator"` and `datasourceTemplate: "github-releases"` (NOT git-tags — flux-operator publishes formal GitHub releases per pinned tag). The packageRules entry mirrors the OpenStack-tags rule (disable major, automerge minor/patch with 3-day minimumReleaseAge).

Data model: a FluxInstance is a single cluster-singleton CR; FluxReport is auto-generated by the operator. No persisted data outside Kubernetes.

Potential pitfalls: (a) Forgetting to add `fluxinstance.yaml` to kustomization.yaml resources — the file would render but not be applied. Mitigated by Task 1.3 kustomize-build test. (b) Race between the operator's CRD install and the FluxInstance apply — kubectl-apply with the install.yaml URL should land the CRD first, but if the CRD is not yet visible to discovery cache, kubectl may reject the FluxInstance with `no matches for kind`. Mitigation: invalidate kubectl discovery cache (`kubectl api-resources > /dev/null`) between the two applies, mirroring the existing pattern at deploy-infra.sh:652. (c) Forgetting that `kubectl annotate` on a non-existent resource exits non-zero — the loop must keep `|| true` semantics for the whole iteration, mirroring today.

Architecture diagram (added because this touches >3 components):

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  hack/deploy-infra.sh                                            |
|                                                                  |
|  Step 1 -- kind create cluster                                   |
|  Step 2 -- kubectl apply install.yaml (flux-operator)            |
|         -- kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base (incl FluxInstance) |
|         -- wait_for_fluxinstance Ready                           |
|  Step 3 -- (already-applied k8s overlay) annotate sources        |
|  Step 4 -- wait_for_helmreleases (unchanged)                     |
|  Steps 5-8 -- unchanged                                          |
+----------------------+-------------------------------------------+
                       |
                       v
+----------------------+-------------------------------------------+
|  flux-system namespace                                           |
|                                                                  |
|  Deployment/flux-operator -- watches FluxInstance/FluxReport     |
|        |                                                         |
|        v                                                         |
|  Deployments: source-controller, kustomize-controller,           |
|               helm-controller, notification-controller           |
|        |                                                         |
|        v                                                         |
|  HelmRepository CRs (annotated by deploy-infra.sh)               |
|        |                                                         |
|        v                                                         |
|  HelmRelease CRs --> infrastructure operator pods                |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

Data flow: deploy-infra.sh writes URL --> kubectl --> flux-operator --> FluxInstance reconcile --> Flux controller Deployments come Ready --> existing kustomize overlay's HelmReleases reconciled --> infra stack ready. No new persistent data; FluxReport status is the only new observable surface.
